name: bemserver-api
documentation:
# BEMServer API

:alt: Latest version
:target: https://pypi.org/project/bemserver-api/
```

:alt: Python versions

```

```{image} https://github.com/BEMServer/bemserver-api/actions/workflows/build-release.yaml/badge.svg
:alt: Build status
:target: https://github.com/BEMServer/bemserver-api/actions/workflows/build-release.yaml
```

```{image} https://results.pre-commit.ci/badge/github/BEMServer/bemserver-api/main.svg
:alt: pre-commit.ci status
:target: https://results.pre-commit.ci/latest/github/BEMServer/bemserver-api/main
```

```{image} https://codecov.io/gh/BEMServer/bemserver-api/branch/main/graph/badge.svg
:alt: Code coverage
:target: https://codecov.io/gh/BEMServer/bemserver-api
```

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/bemserver-docs/badge/
:alt: Documentation
:target: http://bemserver-docs.readthedocs.io/
```

BEMServer is a free Building Energy Management software platform.

Its purpose it to store data collected in buildings and produce useful information such as performance indicators or alerts.

functions.name: bemserver_api.database.init_app
documentation:
Init DB accessor with app

Adds app contextteardown method to close DB session.

functions.name: bemserver_api.resources.timeseries_data.routes.get
documentation:
Get timeseries data

Returns data in either JSON or CSV format.

JSON: Each key is a timeseries ID as string. For each timeseries, values are
passed as {timestamp: value} mappings.

CSV: The first column is the timestamp and each other column is a timeseries data.
Column headers are timeseries IDs.

In both cases, timestamps are timezone aware datetimes.

functions.name: bemserver_api.resources.timeseries_data.routes.get_aggregate
documentation:
Get aggregated timeseries data

Returns data in either JSON or CSV format.

JSON: Each key is a timeseries ID as string. For each timeseries, values are
passed as {timestamp: value} mappings.

CSV: The first column is the timestamp and each other column is a timeseries data.
Column headers are timeseries IDs.

In both cases, timestamps are timezone aware datetimes.

functions.name: bemserver_api.resources.timeseries_data.routes.post
documentation:
Post timeseries data

Loads data in either JSON or CSV format.

JSON: Each key is a timeseries ID as string. For each timeseries, values are
passed as {timestamp: value} mappings.

CSV: The first column is the timestamp and each other column is a timeseries data.
Column headers are timeseries IDs.

In both cases, timestamps are timezone aware datetimes.

functions.name: bemserver_api.resources.timeseries_data.routes.get_for_campaign
documentation:
Get timeseries data for a given campaign

Returns data in either JSON or CSV format.

JSON: Each key is a timeseries name as string. For each timeseries, values are
passed as {timestamp: value} mappings.

CSV: The first column is the timestamp and each other column is a timeseries data.
Column headers are timeseries names.

In both cases, timestamps are timezone aware datetimes.

functions.name: bemserver_api.resources.timeseries_data.routes.get_aggregate_for_campaign
documentation:
Get aggregated timeseries data for a given campaign

Returns data in either JSON or CSV format.

JSON: Each key is a timeseries name as string. For each timeseries, values are
passed as {timestamp: value} mappings.

CSV: The first column is the timestamp and each other column is a timeseries data.
Column headers are timeseries names.

In both cases, timestamps are timezone aware datetimes.

functions.name: bemserver_api.resources.timeseries_data.routes.post_for_campaign

functions.name: bemserver_api.resources.analysis.completeness.routes.get_completeness
documentation:
Get timeseries data completeness

Returns a report about data completeness, involving the number of values
per time bucket and the sample interval.

If the theoretical time interval for a timeseries is not defined in database,
it is inferred from the maximum number of values per bucket.

functions.name: bemserver_api.resources.sites.routes.download_weather_data
documentation:
Download weather data for a site

Download weather data from external web service and store it in DB.
Requires weather timeseries to be defined.

functions.name: bemserver_api.resources.sites.routes.get_degree_days
documentation:
Get degree days for a site

Computes degree days on-the-fly.
Requires air temperature timeseries to be defined.

modules.name: bemserver_api.extensions.integrity_error
documentation:
Integrity error management

Catch integrity errors in resources and return appropriate error code.

classes.name: bemserver_api.extensions.integrity_error.catch_integrity_error
documentation:
Context manager catching integrity errors

Can be used as context manager or decorator.

documentation: Check password and return User instance

methods.name: bemserver_api.extensions.authentication.Auth.login_required
documentation:
Decorator providing authentication and authorization

Uses JWT or HTTPBasicAuth.login_required to authenticate user
Sets CurrentUser context variable to authenticated user for the request
Catches Authorization error and aborts accordingly

classes.name: bemserver_api.extensions.ma_fields.AwareDateTime
documentation:
A timezone aware datetime field

Rejects datetimes that can't be converted to pandas timestamps

classes.name: bemserver_api.extensions.ma_fields.Timezone
documentation:
A timezone field

Validates timezone string is in the list of allowed timezones

classes.name: bemserver_api.extensions.ma_fields.UnitSymbol
documentation:
A unit symbol field

Validates unit is defined

classes.name: bemserver_api.extensions.ma_fields.SortField
documentation:
Field used to specify sort order fields

:param list fields: List of fields to sort upon, by order of priority (the
first field is the first sort key). Each field is a field name, optionally
prefixed with "+" or "-".

functions.name: bemserver_api.extensions.smorest.get_token
documentation:
Get access and refresh tokens

Use login and password to get a pair of access and refresh tokens.

No authentication header needed. Credentials must be passed in request payload.

functions.name: bemserver_api.extensions.smorest.refresh_token
documentation:
Refresh access and refresh tokens

When access token is expired, call this resource using the refresh token to get a
new pair of tokens.

As opposed to all other resources, this resource must be accessed using the refresh
token, not the access token.

classes.name: bemserver_api.extensions.smorest.Schema
documentation: Base Schema class to use in the API

classes.name: bemserver_api.extensions.smorest.AutoSchema
documentation:
Auto Schema class used to load/dump SQLAlchemy objects

The API assumes missing = None/null, so we treat missing fields as None
unless they are read-only.
